brand.name: Vela Skincare
brand.tagline: Science you can trust. Skin you'll love.

act1.title: Audience Discovery
act1.question: Our serum customers read ingredient lists. Body care buyers sniff and squeeze.
act1.context: Vela connects Shopify + Meta + Klaviyo. LCBM analyzes 35K serum customers for body care propensity.

bridge_segment.name: Clean Beauty Crossover
bridge_segment.description: Serum customers who already buy clean body care from Nécessaire, Osea, Drunk Elephant Body
bridge_segment.insight: They'd switch to Vela in a heartbeat if they knew it existed

act1.social_agents_insight: Your serum customers will convert easily — they already trust you. The real opportunity is the self-care ritualist who spends $200/month on bath and body but has never bought a face serum. Body care is your Trojan horse into their routine.

new_audiences.name: Luxury Self-Care Ritualists
new_audiences.psychographics: Bath and body is a sacred ritual, spends $200+/month on self-care, values premium ingredients

new_audiences.name: Post-Workout Skincare
new_audiences.psychographics: Gym-to-shower routine, wants fast-absorbing clean products, efficiency-driven

new_audiences.name: New Moms
new_audiences.psychographics: Ingredient-obsessed for safety, time-poor but self-care starved, gentle and clean non-negotiable
new_audiences.recommended_angle: Safe, gentle, clean — for you and your family

new_audiences.name: Ingredient-Educated Shoppers
new_audiences.psychographics: Reads every ingredient list, follows dermatologists on social, won't buy without knowing what's in it

affinities.name: Dermatologist Content
affinities.reason: Clinical authority is the trust signal

strategies.label: Convert Existing Customers
strategies.description: Focus on the 45% crossover opportunity. Convert serum loyalists to full-body Vela routines with trusted brand messaging.
strategies.target: Existing serum customers + clean beauty crossover segment
strategies.pitch: Complete your Vela routine — now for your body
strategies.risks: Limited new customer acquisition, ceiling at existing base size

strategies.label: Body Care as Acquisition Funnel
strategies.description: Use sensory body care to acquire self-care ritualists who'd never start with a $48 serum. Body care is the entry point.
strategies.target: Luxury Self-Care Ritualists, Post-Workout crowd, New Moms
strategies.pitch: 90-second self-care that changes your entire routine
strategies.risks: New customer acquisition is harder, requires new creative language for Vela

act2.title: Creative Bake-off
act2.question: Ingredient breakdowns won't sell body lotion.
act2.context: 12 creatives submitted. Some are Vela's clinical style, others lean sensory.

creatives.name: Full Routine Carousel
creatives.description: Serum + body oil shown as a morning-to-night complete system
creatives.hook: Your complete Vela ritual — face to body
creatives.why_it_works: Existing customers already trust Vela. Showing the full system drives AOV.
creatives.why_it_fails: New audiences haven't bought the serum — the 'complete routine' means nothing yet.

creatives.name: 90-Second Self-Care
creatives.description: Hands applying body oil, steam, soft lighting, ASMR texture sounds
creatives.hook: 90 seconds. That's all your skin needs.
creatives.why_it_works: Vela's highest-scoring creative ever. Pure sensory appeal for ritualists.
creatives.why_it_fails: Existing customers want ingredient validation, not just aesthetics.

creatives.name: Ingredient Deep-Dive
creatives.description: Clinical breakdown of key actives in body lotion — ceramides, peptides, vitamin E
creatives.hook: What's actually in your body lotion? (Most brands won't tell you)
creatives.why_it_works: Serum customers are ingredient obsessives. This is their love language.
creatives.why_it_fails: Clinical aesthetic actively repels the audience that buys for sensory pleasure.

creatives.name: The Texture Shot
creatives.description: Extreme close-up of oil texture, skin absorption, glow result
creatives.hook: Watch what happens to your skin in 60 seconds
creatives.why_it_works: Visual ASMR drives impulse. Ritualists buy with eyes and skin, not brain.
creatives.why_it_fails: Too product-focused without clinical backing for ingredient-savvy buyers.

creatives.name: Derm Approved
creatives.description: Dermatologist breaks down why the body oil formulation is superior
creatives.hook: A dermatologist reviewed our formula. Here's what she found.
creatives.why_it_works: Medical authority is the ultimate trust signal for Vela's existing base.
creatives.why_it_fails: Self-care ritualists don't need a derm to tell them something feels good.

creatives.name: Sunday Reset Ritual
creatives.description: Full Sunday self-care routine — bath, body oil, face serum, journaling, tea
creatives.hook: This is what a real Sunday reset looks like
creatives.why_it_works: Ritual content is aspirational. Luxury ritualists see themselves in this.
creatives.why_it_fails: Too lifestyle-heavy for existing customers who want product specifics.

cross_sell.landing_page: Full routine page with ingredient breakdown and serum cross-sell

trojan_horse.landing_page: Sensory landing page — texture, scent, feel. No clinical language.
trojan_horse.tiktok_note: 90-Second Self-Care video is tailor-made for TikTok — first TikTok campaign for Vela

act3.title: Performance Diagnosis
act3.question: Body oil is outselling the serum. But something's off with the funnel.
act3.context: Six weeks in. Both tracks are profitable. But the strategic goal isn't being met.

tracks.name: Existing Customers
tracks.insight: High ROAS but limited scale — ceiling is the existing customer base

tracks.name: New Audiences
tracks.insight: Clinical messaging doesn't convert sensory buyers

diagnosis.title: Tonal Mismatch in Post-Purchase
diagnosis.not_the_problem: Acquisition ads are working — both tracks are profitable
diagnosis.root_cause: The post-purchase email sequence immediately pushes the serum with ingredient messaging. But customers acquired through routine content expected a lifestyle brand, not a chemistry class.
diagnosis.funnel_insight: Post-purchase survey: 'I loved the product but felt overwhelmed by the follow-up emails. Too scientific for what I thought was a self-care brand.'
diagnosis.social_agents: You acquired them with a spa vibe and then emailed them a chemistry lesson. Bridge the gap — show them the serum as the next step in the ritual, not a separate clinical product.

recovery_strategies.label: Complete the Ritual
recovery_strategies.description: Rewrite post-purchase sequence. Position serum as the final, elevated step in the self-care ritual — not a separate product.
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: You found your body ritual. Now complete it. Meet the serum that started it all.
recovery_strategies.email_sequence: Day 1: Welcome to the ritual. Day 3: Your body is glowing — your face deserves the same. Day 7: The complete Vela ritual revealed.

recovery_strategies.label: Segment by Purchase Intent
recovery_strategies.description: Split email flows completely. Clinical buyers get ingredient-forward sequence. Sensory buyers get ritual-forward sequence.
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: Clinical path: 'The science behind your glow.' Ritual path: 'Your 3-step ritual is almost complete.'

tracks.name: New Audiences (Body Care Entry)
tracks.insight: Trojan horse is working for acquisition, but serum upsell is underperforming

tracks.name: Existing Customers (Cross-sell)
tracks.insight: Existing customers convert to body care AND come back for more serum

diagnosis.title: Post-Purchase Journey Gap
diagnosis.not_the_problem: The ads are working — body oil is outselling the serum in volume
diagnosis.root_cause: New body care customers were acquired through sensory content but immediately receive clinical serum messaging. The tonal whiplash is killing the upsell.
diagnosis.funnel_insight: Only 4% of new body care buyers add the serum within 30 days. Email open rates drop 60% after Day 3 of post-purchase sequence.
diagnosis.social_agents: You acquired them with a spa vibe and then emailed them a chemistry lesson. Bridge the gap — show them the serum as the next step in the ritual, not a separate product.

recovery_strategies.label: Complete the Ritual
recovery_strategies.description: Rewrite the post-purchase journey. The serum is positioned as 'the final step in the ritual' not a separate clinical product.
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: You found your body ritual. Now complete it.
recovery_strategies.email_sequence: Day 1: Welcome to Vela. Day 3: Your skin is glowing from the outside. Now let's work from within. Day 7: Meet the serum that started everything.

recovery_strategies.label: Bundle as Entry Point
recovery_strategies.description: Create a discovery bundle — body oil + serum sample at $52. Lower commitment, introduces serum organically.
recovery_strategies.transsuasion_copy: Start with the body. Fall in love with the face.

act4.title: Regional Expansion
act4.question: National DTC works. Now we're launching in Sephora in 3 regions.
act4.context: Vela is entering Sephora doors in LA, NYC, and Miami. Each is a different beauty market.

markets.name: Los Angeles
markets.beauty_culture: Clean beauty is mainstream — highly competitive
markets.competitive_landscape: Local clean brands dominate. Clinical positioning is commoditized.
markets.lcbm_insight: Vela's clinical positioning is overcrowded in LA. Lead with the sensory angle that won for new audiences. The 90-Second Self-Care creative adapted for in-store discovery.
markets.sephora_strategy: In-store sampling stations with texture-first experience. QR code to 90-second ritual video.

playbook.awareness: Ritual-forward social content targeting LA wellness culture
playbook.consideration: In-store Sephora discovery — testers and texture experience
playbook.conversion: Bundle offer — body oil + serum sample at launch price

ritual_positioning.advantage: Ritual language perfectly matched to LA wellness culture
ritual_positioning.extra_tactic: Partner with LA wellness studios for sampling events

dual_messaging.advantage: Segment LA ads — clinical for WeHo, sensory for Silver Lake
dual_messaging.extra_tactic: Neighborhood-targeted social ads with different creative per area

markets.name: New York City
markets.competitive_landscape: Saturated premium market but high willingness to pay for proven formulas
markets.lcbm_insight: NYC is the one market where Vela's original clinical positioning resonates with cold audiences. Ingredient-savvy shoppers here do care about ceramides. But premium fatigue is real — emphasize value-per-ounce vs competitors.
markets.sephora_strategy: Ingredient comparison cards in-store. 'What's actually in your body lotion?' shelf talkers.

playbook.awareness: Ingredient education content — why Vela's formula is different
playbook.consideration: Value comparison — cost-per-application vs premium competitors
playbook.conversion: Dermatologist endorsement display + clinical proof points

ritual_positioning.advantage: NYC professionals respond to ritual + efficiency combination
ritual_positioning.extra_tactic: Morning routine content series for NYC commuters

bundle_positioning.advantage: NYC loves a complete routine — bundle displays drive higher AOV
bundle_positioning.extra_tactic: Sephora endcap display featuring complete Vela ritual bundle

markets.name: Miami
markets.competitive_landscape: Tropical skincare brands dominate. Heavy products lose immediately.
markets.lcbm_insight: Humidity-driven concerns mean 'oil' sounds heavy and wrong for Miami. The body oil needs repositioning — 'Weightless hydration that actually works in Miami heat.' Body lotion leads here, not the oil.
markets.sephora_strategy: Climate-specific messaging — 'Made for Miami skin.' Lightweight texture demos in-store.

playbook.awareness: Miami-specific creative — beach, humidity, lightweight skincare
playbook.consideration: Texture videos showing fast absorption in warm weather
playbook.conversion: Lotion leads, oil repositioned as 'weightless glow oil'

ritual_positioning.advantage: Miami's outdoor lifestyle creates a natural post-beach ritual opportunity
ritual_positioning.extra_tactic: Beach recovery ritual campaign — post-sun body oil positioning

dual_messaging.advantage: Split Miami — body lotion for daily use, oil for special occasions
dual_messaging.extra_tactic: Two separate Miami campaigns with humidity-specific messaging

layers.name: Sephora Drive-to-Store
layers.description: Targeted ads driving to nearest Sephora location with in-store offer

layers.name: DTC Retargeting
layers.description: Online browsers retargeted with DTC offer + subscription discount

layers.name: Brand Building
layers.description: City-specific ritual content building Vela as the local clean beauty brand
